final_score_pct = max(0, final_pct − penalty)

_notes.revenue_vs_net_income: This chart shows how much money the company brings in (revenue) versus how much it actually keeps as profit (net income) each year. A healthy company should show both lines growing over time. If revenue grows but net income shrinks, the company is spending more than it is earning — a warning sign worth investigating.
_notes.fcf_vs_debt: Free cash flow is the cash left over after the company has paid for everything it needs to run and grow the business. This chart compares that cash to the company's total debt. A company with rising free cash flow and falling debt is in a strong financial position. If debt is rising faster than cash, the company may be taking on more risk than it can comfortably manage.
_notes.shares_vs_buybacks: When a company buys back its own shares, it reduces the total number of shares in circulation — which means each remaining share represents a larger slice of the business. This chart shows how the share count has changed over time. Consistent buybacks are generally a sign that management believes the stock is undervalued and wants to return cash to shareholders.
_notes.returns_trajectory: Return on Equity (ROE) measures how much profit a company generates for every dollar of shareholder money invested. Return on Invested Capital (ROIC) is similar but also includes debt. Both are expressed as a percentage — higher is better. A company consistently earning above 15% ROIC is generally considered to have a genuine competitive advantage over its peers.
_notes.revenue_vs_ar: Accounts receivable is money the company is owed by customers who have not yet paid their bills. This chart compares revenue growth to accounts receivable growth each year. If accounts receivable is growing much faster than revenue, it may mean the company is struggling to collect payments — sometimes a sign of aggressive or fictitious revenue reporting. A healthy company should see both lines move at a similar pace.

forecast_meta.constraint_note: EPS growth moderated from 0.0% to 2.0% due to financial strength score of 5/10
forecast_meta.growth_explanation: Base (2.0%/yr): growth in line with analyst expectations. Bull (2.6%/yr): everything goes to plan. Bear (1.1%/yr): growth disappoints. Growth capped at 2.0% (from 0.0%) — the balance sheet (financial strength 5/10) limits how fast this company can realistically grow.

bull_points: Q4 2025 adjusted corporate EBITDA margin improved 3.3 percentage points year-over-year, signaling improved operational efficiency and potential positive earnings momentum heading into 2026.

Digital order penetration reached 89.3% of total orders in Q4 2025 (up from 86.1% YoY) with 14.3% growth in monthly transacting customers to 3.43 million, providing a scalable, high-margin revenue stream as the company matures its digital-first strategy.

Stock trades at 67% margin of safety ($2.00 vs. $6.00 intrinsic value), creating asymmetric upside if near-term franchise momentum and cost reduction initiatives drive earnings improvements toward analyst expectations.
bear_points: Total revenues declined 7.3% in 2025 primarily from closure of underperforming stores, and same-store sales fell 2.4% systemwide due to intensified competition and elevated delivery discounts, indicating continued near-term headwinds.

Company-owned store contribution margin compressed to 7% in 2025 from 7.4% in 2024 due to delivery-related costs from aggregator platform dynamics, and management acknowledges aggressive platform subsidies may persist into 2026.

Cash position declined sharply to RMB 129.7 million ($18.5 million) as of December 31, 2025, from RMB 184.2 million a year prior, limiting financial flexibility and raising refinancing risk if 2026 expansion and profitability targets miss.

bull_points: Franchise model shows significant operating leverage, with 485 franchise locations (up from 446) and over 10,000 applications in pipeline; special-channel franchises generate high-teens margins without delivery/discount reliance, positioning THCH for margin expansion as franchise penetration grows.

Management targeting 1-2% further food and packaging cost reductions in 2026 on top of 1.4 percentage-point gains in 2025, creating structural margin improvement opportunity that could offset competitive pressures and drive operating profitability despite negative current profit margin of -33%.

System sales grew 7.6% in 2025 driven by store expansion to 1,047 locations across 92 cities, with at least 100 net new openings planned for 2026; this geographic and unit expansion in underpenetrated China market provides multi-year growth optionality aligned with 2% base growth rate assumption.
bear_points: Financial strength score of 0/10, current ratio of 0.33, and negative profit margins of -33% indicate severe balance sheet stress and operational burn; the company is capital-constrained and dependent on continued debt refinancing ($89.9M convertible notes issued in 2025) to fund store expansion.

Profitability score of 0/10 and null ROE/ROIC metrics reveal the company is unprofitable and not generating shareholder returns despite valuation upside; structural margin recovery must materialize at scale to justify the intrinsic value assumption, representing significant execution risk.

Growth is constrained (moderated from 0% to 2% base rate due to weak financial strength) and predictability is low (score 2/10), creating uncertainty around management's ability to execute 2026 targets amid intensifying competition, delivery margin pressure, and limited financial flexibility to invest in growth initiatives.

company_profile.name: TG Therapeutics, Inc.
company_profile.type_rationale: Standard company — balanced weighting across all methods
company_profile.description: TG Therapeutics, Inc., a commercial stage biopharmaceutical company, focuses on the acquisition, development, and commercialization of novel treatments for B-cell mediated diseases in the United States and internationally. The company provides BRIUMVI, an anti-CD20 monoclonal antibody for the treatment of adult patients with relapsing forms of multiple sclerosis (RMS), including clinically isolated syndrome, relapsing-remitting disease, and active secondary progressive disease in adults. The company's development pipeline comprises Ublituximab IV, glycoengineered anti-CD20 mAb for the treatment of relapsing MS; TG-1701 is an orally available and covalently bound Bruton's tyrosine kinase (BTK) inhibitor that exhibits selectivity to BTK in vitro kinase screening; and TG-1801, a bispecific CD47 and CD19 antibody. Its research pipeline includes various investigational medicines. The company has license agreements with LFB Biotechnologies S.A.S; GTC Biotherapeutics; LFB/GTC LLC; Ildong Pharmaceutical Co. Ltd.; and Jiangsu Hengrui Medicine Co. The company was incorporated in 1993 and is based in Morrisville, North Carolina.
company_profile.description_summary:
TG Therapeutics develops and commercializes treatments for B-cell mediated diseases. It generates revenue from BRIUMVI, an anti-CD20 monoclonal antibody approved for relapsing forms of multiple sclerosis in adults. The company also maintains a pipeline of investigational candidates including oral BTK inhibitors and bispecific antibodies.

The company distinguishes itself through partnerships with international biopharmaceutical firms and licensing agreements, enabling access to diverse therapeutic platforms targeting B-cell disorders beyond multiple sclerosis.

company_profile.type_rationale: Standard company — balanced weighting across all methods
company_profile.description: TryHard Holdings Limited, a lifestyle entertainment company, provides a range of services across the entertainment and hospitality sectors. The company operates through Consultancy and Management, Event Curation, Sub-Leasing, and Restaurants segments. It offers multi-genre event curation services, including conceptualizing an event or function; designing and creating a theme for a stage or event; and provision of public announcement sound systems, lighting, and disc jockey and video jockey technicians, as well as security, graphics/web design, music video production and enhancement, and ticketing services. The company also provides consultancy and management services, such as venue management, artist production, management, security, food and beverage, ticketing, accounting, visual design, VIP packages, photography, logo design, marketing, etc. In addition, it offers sub-leasing solutions to entertainment venue owners and operators; and operates a portfolio of restaurants that provides a range of cuisines and dining experiences. TryHard Holdings Limited was founded in 2013 and is headquartered in Osaka, Japan.
company_profile.description_summary:
This lifestyle entertainment company generates revenue through event curation, consultancy and management services, restaurant operations, and sub-leasing solutions. It offers comprehensive event production including sound systems, lighting, security, and technical staffing, alongside broader venue management and artist representation services.

The company distinguishes itself through diversified operations spanning entertainment services, hospitality, and dining, providing integrated solutions across multiple entertainment sectors.

company_profile.name: Thryv Holdings, Inc.
company_profile.type_rationale: Standard company — balanced weighting across all methods
company_profile.description: Thryv Holdings, Inc. provides digital marketing solutions and cloud-based tools to the small-to-medium-sized businesses in the United States. It operates in two segments, Thryv Marketing Services and Thryv SaaS. The company provides print yellow pages, internet yellow pages, search engine marketing, and other digital media solutions, such as online display and social advertising, online presence and video, and search engine optimization tools. It also offers command center that enables SMBs to centralize all their communication through a modular, expandable, and customizable platform; and business center, a SMB day-to-day business operation, including customer relationship management, appointment scheduling, estimate and invoice creation, payments, document management, social media content, and online review management. In addition, the company provides marketing center, a marketing and advertising platform for AutoID, enhanced online presence, omni-channel paid campaigns, and marketing tools; Thryv Add-ons, which offers AI-assisted website development, SEO tools, google business profile optimization, Thryv Leads, and Hub by Thryv; and ThryvPay, a payment solution that allows users to get paid through credit card and ACH. Further, it provides Keap, a sales and marketing automation engine that helps small businesses. The company was formerly known as Dex Media Holdings, Inc. Thryv Holdings, Inc. was incorporated in 2012 and is headquartered in Grapevine, Texas.
company_profile.description_summary:
Thryv Holdings provides digital marketing solutions and cloud-based software tools designed for small-to-medium-sized businesses. It generates revenue through two main segments: Thryv Marketing Services, which includes print and internet yellow pages, search engine marketing, and digital advertising; and Thryv SaaS, offering business management platforms like command center, business center, and marketing center.

The company differentiates itself through comprehensive integrated solutions. Its platform centralizes communications, manages customer relationships and appointments, handles payments via ThryvPay, and provides marketing automation through Keap. Additional offerings include AI-assisted website development, SEO optimization, and online review management.

company_profile.type_rationale: Asset manager/broker — DNI prioritised; standard CF metrics unreliable
company_profile.description: UP Fintech Holding Limited provides online brokerage services focusing on Chinese investors in New Zealand, the Cayman Island, Singapore, the United States, and internationally. The company has developed a brokerage platform, Tiger Trade which allows investor to trade stocks, options, warrants, and other financial instruments that can be accessed through its APP and website. It also provides value-added services, including investor education, community engagement, and IR/PR platform services. company_profile.description_summary:
UP Fintech operates an online brokerage platform called Tiger Trade, enabling investors to trade stocks, options, warrants, and other financial instruments via mobile app and website. The company generates revenue primarily from brokerage services, trade execution, margin financing, and securities lending, alongside wealth management and asset management offerings.

The company differentiates itself through comprehensive value-added services including investor education, community engagement, and IR/PR platforms, plus specialized offerings like ESOP management, fund structuring, IPO underwriting, and futures trading.

_meta.type_rationale: Asset manager/broker — DNI prioritised; standard CF metrics unreliable
_meta.method_explanation: Asset managers and brokers earn fees for managing other people's money. Their balance sheets hold client assets that do not belong to the firm, which makes standard balance sheet metrics like Price-to-Book misleading. Instead, we focus on net income and revenue-based methods, which better reflect the underlying fee-earning power of the business.

_notes.revenue_vs_net_income: This chart shows how much money the company brings in (revenue) versus how much it actually keeps as profit (net income) each year. A healthy company should show both lines growing over time. If revenue grows but net income shrinks, the company is spending more than it is earning — a warning sign worth investigating.
_notes.fcf_vs_debt: Free cash flow is the cash left over after the company has paid for everything it needs to run and grow the business. This chart compares that cash to the company's total debt. A company with rising free cash flow and falling debt is in a strong financial position. If debt is rising faster than cash, the company may be taking on more risk than it can comfortably manage.
_notes.shares_vs_buybacks: When a company buys back its own shares, it reduces the total number of shares in circulation — which means each remaining share represents a larger slice of the business. This chart shows how the share count has changed over time. Consistent buybacks are generally a sign that management believes the stock is undervalued and wants to return cash to shareholders.
_notes.returns_trajectory: Return on Equity (ROE) measures how much profit a company generates for every dollar of shareholder money invested. Return on Invested Capital (ROIC) is similar but also includes debt. Both are expressed as a percentage — higher is better. A company consistently earning above 15% ROIC is generally considered to have a genuine competitive advantage over its peers.
_notes.revenue_vs_ar: Accounts receivable is money the company is owed by customers who have not yet paid their bills. This chart compares revenue growth to accounts receivable growth each year. If accounts receivable is growing much faster than revenue, it may mean the company is struggling to collect payments — sometimes a sign of aggressive or fictitious revenue reporting. A healthy company should see both lines move at a similar pace.

company_profile.type_rationale: Standard company — balanced weighting across all methods
company_profile.description: Titan Machinery Inc. owns and operates a network of full service agricultural and construction equipment stores in the United States, Europe, and Australia. The company operates through four segments: Agriculture, Construction, Europe, and Australia. It sells new and used equipment, including agricultural and construction equipment manufactured under the CNH Industrial family of brands, as well as equipment from various other manufacturers. The company offers agricultural equipment, including machinery and attachments for use in the production of food, fiber, feed grain, feedstock, and renewable energy; and home and garden applications, as well as maintenance of commercial, residential, and government properties. It also provides construction equipment, such as heavy construction machinery, light industrial machinery for commercial and residential construction, and road and highway construction machinery. In addition, the company offers repair and maintenance services that include warranty repairs, off-site and on-site repair services, scheduling off-season maintenance services, and notifying customers of periodic service requirements; and training programs to customers, as well as sells maintenance and replacement parts. Further, the company rents equipment; and provides ancillary equipment support services, such as equipment transportation, global positioning system signal subscriptions and other precision farming products, farm data management products, and finance and insurance products. It operates in Colorado, Idaho, Iowa, Kansas, Minnesota, Nebraska, North Dakota, South Dakota, Wisconsin, and Wyoming, the United States; Bulgaria, Germany, Romania, and Ukraine, Europe; and New South Wales, South Australia, and Victoria, and Australia. The company was founded in 1980 and is headquartered in West Fargo, North Dakota.

Titan Machinery operates a network of agricultural and construction equipment dealerships across the United States, Europe, and Australia. metric_assessments.profitability: The company is losing money on every dollar of revenue—with a profit margin of -156.7%, expenses far exceed what the business brings in. This isn't a temporary phase; it reflects fundamental challenges in unit economics and cost control. Until Tilray reaches breakeven or positive margins, it cannot build a sustainable business.
metric_assessments.financial_strength: While Tilray maintains a reasonable cash position with a current ratio of 2.46, this masks a serious underlying problem: debt-to-EBITDA of 19.37x is extremely high and unsustainable. The company is burning cash faster than it can service debt, leaving little room for error if market conditions worsen.
metric_assessments.growth: Revenue is contracting at -7.8% annually, and there's no path to earnings growth—the company has zero EPS growth forecast for the next five years. The business is shrinking, not expanding, making it impossible to grow into a healthier financial position or offset current losses.
metric_assessments.predictability: With negative profitability, flat growth expectations, and volatile market dynamics in cannabis, there's almost no way to forecast what this company will earn in future quarters. Investors cannot rely on historical performance to predict what comes next, creating substantial uncertainty about capital returns.
metric_assessments.valuation: The stock trades at a valuation that looks cheap on surface-level metrics, but that's because the business is unprofitable and contracting—cheap often means cheap for a reason. A lower price doesn't create value if the underlying business cannot generate earnings to justify any price.

company_profile.type_rationale: Standard company — balanced weighting across all methods
company_profile.description: Telix Pharmaceuticals Limited, a commercial-stage biopharmaceutical company, focuses on the development and commercialization of therapeutic and diagnostic radiopharmaceuticals. The company operates through three segments: Precision Medicine, Therapeutics, and Manufacturing Solutions. Its lead therapeutic product candidate is TLX591, a lutetium-labeled radio antibody-drug conjugate (rADC), which is in a Phase 3 clinical trial in patients with advanced prostate cancer. The company develops TLX250, an rADC for treating advanced metastatic kidney cancer; TLX101, a systemic therapy for the treatment of glioblastoma; TLX66, a product candidate for bone marrow conditioning for hematopoietic stem cell transplant conditioning; and Illuccix and Gozellix for the treatment of prostate cancer. The company is also developing TLX592, a prostate cancer therapy candidate for targeted alpha therapy based on its proprietary RADmAb-engineered antibody technology; TLX252 for the treatment of patients with advanced metastatic kidney cancer; TLX400, a bladder fibroblast activation protein for treating various tumors; TLX102, a large amino acid transporter-targeting small molecule-based alpha therapy candidate for the treatment of glioblastoma and multiple myeloma; TLX300 for treating soft tissue sarcoma; and TLX090, a bone-seeking agent for bone metastases and pain palliation. In addition, it is developing BiPASS, which is in Phase 3 clinical trial for prostate cancer diagnosis; AlFluor, a novel PET radiochemistry solution; TLX250-Px, a PET diagnostic imaging agent; and TLX101-Px, a radiolabeled amino acid PET agent. It operates in Australia, Belgium, Canada, the United Kingdom, the United States, and internationally. The company has strategic collaboration with University Hospital Essen. The company was founded in 2015 and is headquartered in North Melbourne, Australia.
company_profile.description_summary:
Telix Pharmaceuticals develops and commercializes therapeutic and diagnostic radiopharmaceuticals across three segments: Precision Medicine, Therapeutics, and Manufacturing Solutions. The company's pipeline includes lead candidates like TLX591 for prostate cancer and TLX250 for kidney cancer, alongside diagnostic products including Illuccix and Gozellix.

The company distinguishes itself through proprietary radiopharmaceutical technologies, including its RADmAb-engineered antibody platform for targeted alpha therapy and novel PET radiochemistry solutions, addressing oncology and diagnostic imaging needs across multiple tumor types.

company_profile.description: TransMedics Group, Inc., a commercial-stage medical technology company, engages in transforming organ transplant therapy for end-stage organ failure patients in the United States and internationally. Its OCS includes OCS LUNG for the preservation of standard criteria donor lungs for double-lung transplantation; OCS Heart, a technology for preservation of DBD donor hearts deemed unsuitable due to limitations of cold storage and for ex vivo reanimation, functional monitoring, and beating-heart preservation of donation-after-circulatory-death hearts; and OCS Liver for the preservation of DBD and DCD of donor livers. The company also developed national OCS program, a turnkey solution for outsourced organ retrieval; and provides OCS organ management and logistics services, including aviation and ground transportation, and other coordination activity. The company was founded in 1998 and is headquartered in Andover, Massachusetts.
company_profile.description_summary:
TransMedics develops the Organ Care System, a portable technology that preserves and optimizes donor organs outside the body under near-physiologic conditions. The company generates revenue from its OCS platforms for lung, heart, and liver transplantation, along with organ management and logistics services including retrieval and transportation.

The technology enables preservation of organs previously unsuitable for transplant and allows functional monitoring and reanimation of donor hearts, expanding the pool of viable organs for end-stage patients.

_meta.type_rationale: FCF compounder — FCF margin 17.1%; DCF methods prioritised
_meta.method_explanation: Free cash flow compounders are businesses that consistently generate more cash than they need to run the business — think of companies like consumer goods brands or software firms with subscription revenue. Because their cash flows are reliable year after year, a Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) model works very well. DCF asks: if I add up all the future cash flows this company will generate and adjust for the time value of money, what is it worth today? For these companies, that calculation gives a meaningful and trustworthy answer.

quality_narrative.moat_rationale: T-Mobile has built a moderate competitive moat through solid profitability and predictable cash flows, but weak returns on invested capital and high debt limit its ability to reinvest profits and defend its position against larger rivals.

metric_assessments.profitability: T-Mobile's 11.6% profit margin is above-average for telecom companies, where margins typically range 8–13%, showing the company extracts decent returns from each dollar of revenue. The business is generating real profits on a strong revenue base, which proves the core model works and gives management real cash to work with. However, the margin sits in the middle of the industry range rather than at the top, indicating less pricing power or operational efficiency than the best-in-class competitors.
metric_assessments.financial_strength: With a current ratio of 1.0 and debt-to-EBITDA of 3.6x, T-Mobile has just enough short-term liquidity to cover immediate bills but no cushion for unexpected shocks—a current ratio below 1.2 is tight for a large utility-like business. The 3.6x leverage ratio is materially above the 2–2.5x considered healthy for telecom, which means the company is carrying more debt than a balanced structure would recommend and has less room to weather downturns or invest in new technology. This is the primary constraint on the financial strength score and explains why the company cannot pursue aggressive growth strategies without financial risk.
metric_assessments.growth: Revenue growth of 10.6% annually is solid and above the 3–5% typical for mature telecom networks, indicating T-Mobile is still gaining market share or pricing power in a maturing industry. Projected 5-year EPS growth of 19.9% suggests earnings will expand faster than revenue, likely from margin improvements or share buybacks rather than core business acceleration. The score is capped at 5 because growth is constrained—the company operates in a limited market where subscriber bases plateau and competition is intense, meaning sustained double-digit growth becomes harder each year as the base gets larger.
metric_assessments.predictability: An 18% return on equity and a 7/10 predictability score reflect a business with stable, recurring revenue streams and consistent cash generation that Wall Street can forecast with reasonable accuracy. Telecom customers are sticky; they do not frequently switch providers, and pricing adjusts slowly, making quarterly results fairly forecastable quarter to quarter. The score is not higher because absolute ROIC of 7.48% is below the 12–15% threshold for highly predictable businesses, signaling that while cash flows are reliable, the company is not generating outsized returns that would make future performance obvious and certain.
metric_assessments.valuation: At a 7/10 valuation score, T-Mobile appears fairly valued to modestly undervalued, meaning the stock price reasonably reflects the company's current earnings power and growth trajectory without significant upside or downside surprise. The combined signal of good profitability, adequate growth, and predictable cash flows—alongside moderate leverage—supports a fair market price rather than a bargain or overvaluation. This score does not suggest the stock is cheap in absolute terms; rather, it indicates an investor would not overpay or get a steal at current levels.

quality_narrative.classification_reason: T-Mobile is rated Speculative because while the business is profitable and generates predictable cash flows, the company carries elevated debt (3.6x EBITDA) that constrains financial flexibility and growth potential, and operates in a mature market with limited room for expansion. This combination means earnings could be vulnerable if competition intensifies or the economy softens, making the stock suitable for investors comfortable with moderate risk rather than those seeking stable, defensive holdings.
quality_narrative.investment_narrative: T-Mobile runs a solid, cash-generative telecom business with good profitability and market-share momentum that shows up in 10.6% revenue growth—substantially faster than the industry average—and strong 19.9% projected 5-year EPS growth. However, the company carries 3.6x debt-to-EBITDA, which is above the comfort zone for telecom and leaves little financial flexibility to invest in 5G buildout, acquire competitors, or weather a downturn without stress. The core risk is that growth will slow as the subscriber base matures and competition remains fierce, while debt levels prevent aggressive action to maintain momentum. Valuation looks fair at current levels—you are not buying a discount, but you are not overpaying either—and the 18% ROE shows management is putting shareholder capital to reasonable use. For investors seeking reliable telecom exposure with modest growth and acceptable risk, T-Mobile is a reasonable core holding, but it is not a compelling bargain and carries more balance-sheet risk than larger peers.

company_profile.name: TOP Financial Group Limited
company_profile.type_rationale: Asset manager/broker — DNI prioritised; standard CF metrics unreliable
company_profile.description: TOP Financial Group Limited, through its subsidiaries, operates as an online brokerage company in Hong Kong. The company is involved in the trading of equities, futures, and options products; and the provision of brokerage and value-added services, including trade order placement and execution, account management, and customer support services. It also provides a range of futures products, such as index, forex, agricultural product, energy, and precious metal futures; and operates as an escrow agent in transactions relating to securities sales and transfers. In addition, the company offers stock and options brokerage, structured note subscriber, consulting, currency exchange, margin financing, OTC derivative trading, and loan services. The company was formerly known as Zhong Yang Financial Group Limited and changed its name to TOP Financial Group Limited in July 2022. The company was founded in 2015 and is headquartered in Singapore. TOP Financial Group Limited is a subsidiary of Zhong Yang Holdings (BVI) Limited.
company_profile.description_summary:
TOP Financial Group operates as an online brokerage company in Hong Kong, primarily generating revenue through trading services in equities, futures, and options. It provides comprehensive brokerage services including trade execution, account management, and customer support, alongside margin financing, OTC derivatives, and loan services.

The company distinguishes itself through diverse product offerings spanning index, forex, agricultural, energy, and precious metal futures, plus structured notes and currency exchange services. It also functions as an escrow agent for securities transactions.

_meta.type_rationale: Asset manager/broker — DNI prioritised; standard CF metrics unreliable
_meta.method_explanation: Asset managers and brokers earn fees for managing other people's money. Their balance sheets hold client assets that do not belong to the firm, which makes standard balance sheet metrics like Price-to-Book misleading. Instead, we focus on net income and revenue-based methods, which better reflect the underlying fee-earning power of the business.

company_profile.description: Tuniu Corporation, together with its subsidiaries, operates as an online leisure travel company in the People's Republic of China. The company offers various packaged tours, including organized tours that provide pre-arranged itineraries, transportation, accommodation, entertainment, meals, and tour guide services; and self-guided tours consist of flights and hotel bookings, and other optional add-ons. It also sells tourist attraction tickets; and provides visa applications, hotel booking, air ticketing, train ticketing, car rental, and insurance services. company_profile.description_summary:
This online leisure travel company operates throughout China, generating revenue primarily through packaged tours with pre-arranged itineraries and self-guided tours combining flights and accommodations. It also sells attraction tickets and provides ancillary services including visa applications, hotel bookings, air ticketing, and insurance.

The company distinguishes itself through its omnichannel distribution model, serving customers via its website, mobile platform, call centers, and physical retail stores across China. Additionally, it generates revenue from advertising services to tourism boards and bureaus.
